title: [Notary Seal]
content:
[Notary Seal]

---

Instructions for Use:

1. Fill in the Blanks: Complete the date, state, county, names of the signatories, and the type of identification used for verification.
2. Signature by the Parties: Ensure both the Service Provider and the Client sign the SOW document in the presence of the notary.
3. Notary Acknowledgment: The notary public will then fill in their part, confirming the identity of the signatories and witnessing the signing. They will also affix their official seal.
4. Check Local Requirements: Notary requirements can vary by location, so it's advisable to check any specific requirements in your state or country to ensure compliance.

Adding this notarization section will enhance the legal robustness of your SOW, providing an additional layer of verification and formal acknowledgment of the agreement.
